tests.source_program:
declare void @use(i32)

define void @test_unsigned_inner_cmp(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %eq = icmp eq i64 %a, %b
  %ugt = icmp ugt i64 %a, %b
  %. = select i1 %ugt, i32 1, i32 -1
  %result = select i1 %eq, i32 0, i32 %.
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %result, 1
  br i1 %cmp, label %unreached, label %normal

normal:
  ret void

unreached:
  call void @use(i32 %result)
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare void @use(i32)

define void @test_unsigned_inner_cmp(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %result = call i32 @llvm.ucmp.i32(i64 %a, i64 %b)
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %result, 1
  br i1 %cmp, label %unreached, label %normal

normal:
  ret void

unreached:
  call void @use(i32 %result)
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions unsigned support but all existing test examples use signed comparisons (slt). This test uses unsigned predicates (ugt) in the inner select, exercising the ucmp intrinsic path not shown in example tests.

  %cmp.not = icmp ugt i64 %a, %b

normal:                                           ; preds = %0

unreached:                                        ; preds = %0

  call void @use(i32 %result)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i32)

define void @test_unsigned_inner_cmp(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
#0:
  %cmp.not = icmp ugt i64 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp.not, label %normal, label %unreached

unreached:
  %result = ucmp i32 i64 %a, %b
  %#range_0_%result = !range i32 %result, i32 4294967295, i32 2
  call void @use(i32 %#range_0_%result)
  ret void

normal:
  ret void
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
declare void @use(i32)

define void @test_swapped_inner_constants(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %eq = icmp eq i64 %a, %b
  %slt = icmp slt i64 %a, %b
  %. = select i1 %slt, i32 1, i32 -1
  %result = select i1 %eq, i32 0, i32 %.
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %result, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %unreached, label %normal

normal:
  ret void

unreached:
  call void @use(i32 %result)
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare void @use(i32)

define void @test_swapped_inner_constants(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
  %result = call i32 @llvm.scmp.i32(i64 %a, i64 %b)
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %result, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %unreached, label %normal

normal:
  ret void

unreached:
  call void @use(i32 %result)
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The inner select has swapped constants (slt ? 1 : -1) instead of the canonical (slt ? -1 : 1). The patch handles this via InnerTV/InnerFV swap logic, but this specific constant ordering is not demonstrated in existing test examples.

tests.source_program:
declare void @use(i32)

define void @test_i32_operands_narrow_width(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %eq = icmp eq i32 %a, %b
  %sgt = icmp sgt i32 %a, %b
  %. = select i1 %sgt, i32 1, i32 -1
  %result = select i1 %eq, i32 0, i32 %.
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %result, -1
  br i1 %cmp, label %unreached, label %normal

normal:
  ret void

unreached:
  call void @use(i32 %result)
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare void @use(i32)

define void @test_i32_operands_narrow_width(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
  %result = call i32 @llvm.scmp.i32(i32 %a, i32 %b)
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %result, -1
  br i1 %cmp, label %unreached, label %normal

normal:
  ret void

unreached:
  call void @use(i32 %result)
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: All existing test examples use i64 operands with i32 result. This test uses i32 operands directly (narrower width), and uses sgt predicate in the inner select instead of slt. The combination of operand width and predicate variation is not covered in example tests.
